items.reason: All 20 decisions DEC-001 through DEC-020 exist in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` with id, question, options_considered, choice, consequences (naming what's removed/eliminated), and projects_to lists. The required new areas — dictionary generation direction (DEC-016), cross-spec field reconciliation (DEC-017), allowed-value unification (DEC-018), privacy-class closure (DEC-019), and migration generation (DEC-020) — are all present with the six required fields. `decisions-needed.md` enumerates each area and maps it to a real decision id.

`docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md` contains PEND-001 (target 2026-05-15) and PEND-005 (target 2026-05-03) — both have owner (Andy), target_date, blocker, and one-line rationale. Both target dates are future relative to the research date 2026-04-26. Format matches the requirement.

Every decision's consequences section includes at least one bullet starting with 'Removes the need for', 'Eliminates', or 'Lets X be derived' that names a concrete artifact. E.g., DEC-001: 'Removes the need for every roster, launch, analytics, and credential API to invent its own identity merge rules.' DEC-016: 'Removes five-way hand-edited dictionary reconciliation.' DEC-019: 'Eliminates depends_on_entity and depends_on_contents from generated dictionaries.'

Decisions consistently list three credible options bracketing the design space. DEC-001 considers separate per-spec tables, total collapse into one person, and the chosen middle. DEC-016 considers shared-only with on-demand generation, per-spec canonical, and shared+committed projections — all real architectural choices. DEC-017 brackets canonical-only-with-adapters vs preserve-only-native vs the chosen hybrid. None read as strawmen.

DEC-016 declares the shared dictionary upstream and per-spec dictionaries as generated projections, but the implementation contradicts this. `scripts/generate_oneroster_core.py` reads `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json` (the per-spec file) directly, not from `data/data-dictionary.seed.json`. No `scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py` exists in the evidence. The per-spec dictionaries (oneroster-core, qti-core, case-core, caliper-core, integration-governance-core) are hand-edited canonical sources, not generated projections. The substance check explicitly fails when the shared dictionary is a stub against richer per-spec files.

DEC-017 declares that overlapping spec fields must carry `canonical_field_id`, but no per-spec dictionary field in the evidence contains a `canonical_field_id` key. For example, `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json` person.email, `dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json` lti_membership.email, and `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json` caliper_actor.identifier_value have no `canonical_field_id` mapping to a shared dictionary field. The overlap is resolved only in DEC-prose, which the substance check explicitly fails.

No per-spec identity object (OneRoster `person`, Caliper `caliper_actor`, LTI `lti_membership`) carries a `canonical_object_id` field pointing to a shared canonical identity entity. `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is referenced in README but there is no canonical identity object record visible in evidence with fields, allowed values, and projections. DEC-001 describes the unification in prose only; the substance check explicitly fails when unification exists 'only in DEC-001 prose'.

Per-spec enums are declared independently with inline `allowed_values` arrays, not via `shared_enum_id` references to a global enum table. E.g., OneRoster person.primary_role lists [student, teacher, administrator, guardian] inline; LTI roles, Caliper roles, and OneRoster enrollment.role do not crosswalk to a single shared enum. There is no shared enum dictionary with bidirectional crosswalks visible. DEC-018 declares this should happen but the data doesn't implement it.

DEC-019 explicitly forbids `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents`, but `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json` directly contains them: caliper_entity.name has `"privacy_class": "depends_on_entity"`, caliper_entity.description has `"privacy_class": "depends_on_entity"`, and caliper_entity.extensions has `"privacy_class": "depends_on_contents"`. The decision and its enforcement contradict each other in the artifact.

Dictionary objects do not carry a `relationships` array declaring FK targets and cardinality. `supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql` is a hand-written 460-line SQL file with FK declarations (`references public.organizations(id)`) that exist independently of the dictionary. No generator produces migrations from the dictionary's relational graph. DEC-020 declares this should happen but the implementation hasn't followed.

Each per-spec field carries `decision_id` (e.g., person.email has `"decision_id": "DEC-001-person-agent-subject"`), and `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` validates these references against the decision register. However, dictionary objects themselves do not carry an object-level `decision_id`, only a `sourceStandard`. Allowed values and privacy classes also lack explicit decision_id citations — privacy classes are referenced as enum strings only. Field-level coverage exists but object/enum/privacy-class coverage is incomplete.

Generators produce dictionary pages from per-spec sources (`scripts/generate_oneroster_core.py` writes `docs/generated/oneroster-core-dictionary.md` and `site/docs/oneroster-core-dictionary.html`), and `check_dictionary_artifacts.py` verifies the pages match the dictionary. However, prose pages like `docs/lead-spec-accounting.md`, `docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md`, and `docs/spec-gap-backlog.md` are hand-edited and contain load-bearing claims (specific field names, ledger entries) that are not anchored to dictionary entries. The portal landing's API/dictionary tables also include hand-written prose. Generated coverage exists but is not comprehensive.

The decision register renders to HTML at `site/docs/decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html` and the portal's decisions section renders DEC cards from the canonical register with anchors. However, individual decisions like DEC-016 through DEC-020 don't have corresponding plain-language doc pages explaining the why in product terms — they exist only as bullet entries in the register. The substance check warns that 'a link to a 900-line raw Markdown file as the only explanation fails'.

No `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` or equivalent end-to-end buildability guide exists in the evidence. The docs index in `site/docs/index.html` lists feedback-package, supabase-hosted-database, plan, decisions, dictionaries, and accounting pages — but no walkthrough that covers (a) creating a school, (b) enrolling a student, (c) posting a grade, (d) linking to a CASE standard, (e) reading a Caliper activity stream as one connected guide with copy-pasteable curl/SQL.

`site/docs/index.html` enumerates all rendered doc pages and the portal landing links to many of them. However, no link checker output is in the evidence, and `site/index.html` contains href attributes like `docs/generated-oneroster-core-dictionary.html` and `docs/decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html` that need verification against actual file paths. Cannot confirm zero 404s or three-click reachability without a link-check report.

Without a buildability guide, a layperson cannot read only `docs/` and produce executable artifacts for the five-step scenario. Specific gaps: (1) no doc walks through creating a school org with curl/SQL — traces to missing `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md`; (2) the dictionary doesn't unify identity via canonical_object_id, so the developer must reconcile OneRoster Person, Caliper Actor, and LTI Membership manually — traces to dictionary_unifies_identity; (3) CASE alignment for an assignment has no runnable endpoint — traces to DEC-012 deferred runtime; (4) Caliper activity feed reads aren't supported, only authenticated event ingestion receipts — traces to DEC-012 partial Caliper runtime; (5) email/role overlap requires manual cross-spec mapping — traces to dictionary_resolves_cross_spec_overlaps and dictionary_global_enums.

The verdict schema in `scripts/evaluate_platform.py` outputs `{ id, status, reason, evidence_paths, blocked_prerequisites }` per item — there is no `traced_cause` field, and no `buildability_gaps` array is emitted in the report. The build_evaluator_prompt instructions tell the LLM to cite paths but do not require a structured traced_cause referencing a lower-layer artifact. Loop-overrides explicitly say 'Update scripts/evaluate_platform.py to emit a traced_cause field on every non-pass item and a buildability_gaps array' — this hasn't been done.

`VERIFY.md` ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`, and `scripts/codex_loop.py` calls `run_evaluator()` both before and after each iteration. The evaluator writes per-item status, counts, and done flag. However, the report does not include `traced_cause` per non-pass item or a `buildability_gaps` array (see evaluator_traces_failures_backward). The substance check explicitly fails when 'Report missing traced_cause on a non-pass item'.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` calls `read_overrides(repo)` at the start of each iteration before running Codex, has `overrides_paused(overrides)` that returns True when a PAUSE token sits on its own line under the Pause section (checked before the next iteration runs), and `format_overrides_for_prompt` injects the cleaned overrides text into `build_prompt` under the literal 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md`' section. The PAUSE check halts before the next iteration: 'Halting before iteration. Clear the PAUSE token to resume.'

`scripts/codex_loop.py` checks `if after_eval and after_eval.get("done"): print("Evaluator reports `done` after this iteration. Stopping the loop."); return 0`. The loop also has hard caps (DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS=25) and stall detection as safety rails. Termination references the LLM evaluator's `done` flag rather than numeric thresholds; the advisory score gate is explicitly described as 'advisory only' and does not gate publish.

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. QTI and CASE are doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated Edge Function receipt/governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, Caliper raw-event projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.
